meta.originalQuestion: Design a greenfield rewrite plan for judge-gym that keeps Convex as the domain/UI store, moves execution orchestration to self-hosted Temporal, preserves simple operator controls like create/start window and run processes with pause_after and observability, supports agentic continuous monitoring/fix loops similar to v3-finish-pass, and identifies which current tables and abstractions should be kept, removed, or replaced.
pass7Focus: Upstash key vocabulary from normalized quota dimensions
pass10Focus: Convex to Temporal worker API boundary

assignments.title: Architecture split and primitive mapping
assignments.scope: Map current Convex responsibilities to Temporal workflows, activities, workers, task queues, and retained Convex domain state.

assignments.title: Self-hosted Temporal deployment and package layout
assignments.scope: Assess self-hosted Temporal topology, local/dev workflows, TypeScript SDK shape, and how to colocate Temporal code in packages/engine.

assignments.title: Observability and agentic monitoring loop
assignments.scope: Design the live control surface across Temporal, Convex, Axiom, and MCP for continuous monitor/fix loops similar to v3-finish-pass.

assignments.title: Migration strategy and table ownership
assignments.scope: Identify which current Convex tables, APIs, and abstractions should be kept, deleted, mirrored, or replaced in a greenfield rewrite.

assignments.title: Activity idempotency and audit ledger design
assignments.scope: Determine what guarantees Temporal does and does not provide for Activities, and define the replacement for runtime-shaped llm_requests behavior.

assignments.title: Workflow/activity breakdown and control semantics
assignments.scope: Define the simplest first-cut RunWorkflow and WindowWorkflow surface, control messages, and continue-as-new pressure points.

assignments.title: Start consistency and Convex projection boundary
assignments.scope: Determine how to avoid split-brain between Temporal execution truth and Convex product projections.

assignments.title: Observability truth and control plane
assignments.scope: Define what Visibility can and cannot do, what should be mirrored into Convex, and how Axiom/metrics should fit.

assignments.title: Runtime/versioning constraints and option pressure
assignments.scope: Investigate Node vs Bun, Worker Versioning, continue-as-new, and whether Restate or Inngest materially improve the rewrite fit.

assignments.title: Action taxonomy and control contract
assignments.scope: Turn Temporal message primitives into a literal operator and agent control contract.

assignments.title: Provider-aware LLM attempt ledger
assignments.scope: Define exact ledger fields, OpenAI chat and batch semantics, usage/cost fields, and payload retention strategy.

assignments.title: Per-flow start handoff matrix
assignments.scope: Compare create-then-start, start-then-project, with-start, and outbox patterns and map them to judge-gym flows.

assignments.title: Observability truth stack and projection schema
assignments.scope: Define the precedence order among Visibility, Describe, Update receipts, Query, Convex projection, and Axiom telemetry.

assignments.title: Versioning and replay workflow
assignments.scope: Define a pragmatic replay-testing, continue-as-new, and rollout/versioning workflow for a small team.

assignments.title: Requirements-based alternatives matrix
assignments.scope: Compare Temporal, Restate, and Inngest against judge-gym requirements and make rejection criteria explicit.

assignments.title: Settings and config flow
assignments.scope: Define how shared defaults, Convex-stored operator policy, policy snapshots, and runtime-local secrets should interact after the rewrite.

assignments.title: Monorepo package and runtime boundary
assignments.scope: Determine the right package split for Bun, Convex, and Node-only Temporal workers and define what shared code is allowed to contain.

assignments.title: Execution policy, rate limiting, and tooling boundary
assignments.scope: Decide where provider-facing rate limiting, adapter execution, and Temporal-facing operational tooling should live, and identify the remaining cross-worker quota questions.

assignments.title: Global provider quota strategy
assignments.scope: Decide how Temporal queue-level shaping, worker concurrency, and Redis-backed shared buckets should combine for multi-worker provider quotas.

assignments.title: Provider capability divergence
assignments.scope: Capture the real quota, caching, tool, and optional batch differences across OpenAI, Anthropic, and Gemini that the rewrite must model explicitly.

assignments.title: Provider-portable code architecture
assignments.scope: Define a maintainable registry-plus-adapter architecture so the core runtime stays provider-portable without implementing every provider-specific feature in v0.

assignments.title: Minimal v0 capability registry schema
assignments.scope: Define the minimal first-class registry fields the core runtime, llm_attempts envelope, and Upstash quota keys should depend on.

assignments.title: llm_attempts envelope and Upstash key model
assignments.scope: Define the generic top-level attempt envelope, the `provider_extensions` boundary, and the normalized key vocabulary for Upstash quota enforcement.

assignments.title: V0 quota-dimension and tracking split
assignments.scope: Define the normalized quota-dimension vocabulary v0 should support and distinguish which usage fields are tracked but not independently enforced.

assignments.title: Upstash v0 settlement policy
assignments.scope: Define the default Upstash key form, algorithm family, and reservation/reconciliation rules for the v0 quota engine.

assignments.title: Initial provider-to-dimension mapping
assignments.scope: Freeze the first registry snapshot that maps OpenAI, Anthropic, and Gemini onto the normalized v0 quota dimensions without inventing unsupported buckets.

assignments.title: Provider-aware output reservation policy
assignments.scope: Decide how output reservation should differ across providers so the shared limiter follows documented quota semantics rather than one generic heuristic.

assignments.title: Final control contract
assignments.scope: Freeze the exact action contract for create/start/pause_after/pause/resume/cancel/repair, including primitive choice, acknowledgement semantics, idempotency, and fallback behavior.

assignments.title: llm_attempts schema and retention contract
assignments.scope: Freeze the metadata-first ledger shape, payload/blob strategy, quota-audit shape, and replay/retention contract for the rewrite.

assignments.title: Convex to Temporal worker API boundary
assignments.scope: Freeze how Temporal workers talk to Convex, what writes are allowed, and how the package/runtime boundary is enforced.

assignments.title: Observability projection and repair-read model
assignments.scope: Freeze the compact Convex projection, truth precedence, and the rule for what the future agent loop may act on directly.

assignments.title: Safe deployment and versioning SOP
assignments.scope: Freeze the minimum viable replay, patching, continue-as-new, and rollout workflow for the Temporal rewrite.

entries.title: Current Convex Engine Already Reimplements a Workflow Runtime
Temporal Fits the Missing Execution Layer
Self-Hosted Temporal Can Live Beside Convex in packages/engine
Original Observability Split Draft
Table Ownership Split for a Greenfield Rewrite
Convex Scheduling Guarantees Are Strong but Misaligned With This Workload
Activity Idempotency and an External Audit Ledger Are Core Rewrite Requirements
Minimal Workflow Surface Should Stay Small and Use Explicit Control Semantics
Cross-System Start Consistency Requires an Explicit Handoff Pattern
Observability Needs a Split Between Discovery, Strong Truth, and Telemetry
Temporal Remains the Strongest Default, but Runtime and Versioning Constraints Are Real
Control Contract Needs an Explicit Action Taxonomy
The LLM Attempt Ledger Must Capture Provider Semantics, Not Just Temporal Retries
Start Handoff Must Be Chosen Per Flow, Not by One Global Rule
Observability Needs a Precedence Order, Not Just a Split
Versioning and Replay Testing Need an Operational Workflow, Not Just Awareness
Temporal Still Leads, but the Alternatives Need Explicit Rejection Criteria
Settings and Config Flow Should Be Split Into Defaults, Operator Policy, and Runtime Secrets
Monorepo Boundaries Should Follow Runtime Ownership, Not Just Features
Execution Policy, Rate Limiting, and Operational Tooling Should Follow the Worker Runtime
Global Provider Rate Limiting Needs a Layered Design: Temporal for Dispatch, Redis for Shared Quotas
Multi-Provider Support Requires a Capability Matrix, Not an OpenAI-Shaped Core
Provider-Portable Maintainability Depends on Layered Adapters, Capability Metadata, and Extension Fields
The Minimal V0 Capability Registry Should Normalize Identity, Operations, Usage Fields, and Quota Dimensions
Provider-Portable `llm_attempts` and Upstash Keys Should Share the Same Normalized Vocabulary
V0 Should Enforce a Small Quota Enum and Track Richer Usage Separately
Upstash V0 Should Use Token Buckets With Explicit Reservation and Reconciliation Rules
The Initial Registry Snapshot Should Map Provider Quota Dimensions Explicitly
V0 Output Reservation Should Follow Provider-Documented Quota Semantics
The Rewrite Needs a Literal Control Contract With Different Primitives Per Action
The LLM Attempt Ledger Should Be Metadata-First, Append-Only, and Split Across Envelope, Payload, and Quota Event Shapes
Temporal Workers Should Use a Narrow Public Convex Worker API, Not Convex Internals as an RPC Surface
The Observability Read Model Should Stay Small, Non-Authoritative, and Optimized for Discovery and Triage
The Rewrite Needs a Safe-Deployment SOP, Not Just General Awareness of Replay and Versioning

areasOfAnalysis.title: Architecture split and primitive mapping
areasOfAnalysis.question: What is the cleanest ownership split between Convex and Temporal for judge-gym's run/window engine?

areasOfAnalysis.title: Self-hosted Temporal deployment and package layout
areasOfAnalysis.question: How should a self-hosted Temporal stack and engine-local package layout be organized for a TypeScript/Bun repo?

areasOfAnalysis.title: Observability and agentic monitoring loop
areasOfAnalysis.question: How should observability, operator control, and MCP-driven monitoring/fix loops work after the Temporal rewrite?

areasOfAnalysis.title: Migration strategy and table ownership
areasOfAnalysis.question: Which current Convex tables and abstractions remain as domain truth and which should disappear into Temporal runtime concerns?

areasOfAnalysis.title: Activity idempotency and audit ledger design
areasOfAnalysis.question: How should idempotency keys, LLM attempt auditing, and payload retention work once Activities own side effects?

areasOfAnalysis.title: Workflow/activity breakdown and control semantics
areasOfAnalysis.question: What is the smallest safe RunWorkflow/WindowWorkflow surface and how should pause/resume/cancel/repair map to Temporal primitives?

areasOfAnalysis.title: Start consistency and Convex projection boundary
areasOfAnalysis.question: How should judge-gym handle create/start flows and projection writes when Convex and Temporal cannot share an atomic transaction?

areasOfAnalysis.title: Observability truth and control plane
areasOfAnalysis.question: What should be used for discovery, strong execution truth, projected status, and deep telemetry in the future agent loop?

areasOfAnalysis.title: Runtime/versioning constraints and option pressure
areasOfAnalysis.question: How should Node vs Bun, Worker Versioning, continue-as-new, and alternative runtimes affect the rewrite plan?

areasOfAnalysis.title: Action taxonomy and control contract
areasOfAnalysis.question: What exact Temporal primitive, acknowledgement mode, and idempotency rule should each operator and agent action use?

areasOfAnalysis.title: Provider-aware LLM attempt ledger
areasOfAnalysis.question: What exact fields, provider IDs, retention classes, and replay metadata should replace runtime-shaped llm_requests behavior?

areasOfAnalysis.title: Per-flow start handoff matrix
areasOfAnalysis.question: Which create/start flows should use create-then-start, start-then-project, with-start, or outbox patterns?

areasOfAnalysis.title: Observability truth stack and projection schema
areasOfAnalysis.question: What is the exact precedence order and compact Convex projection shape for the future agent loop?

areasOfAnalysis.title: Versioning and replay workflow
areasOfAnalysis.question: What is the minimum viable deployment, replay-testing, and continue-as-new policy for judge-gym workers?

areasOfAnalysis.title: Requirements-based alternatives matrix
areasOfAnalysis.question: Why does Temporal beat or fail to beat Restate and Inngest against judge-gym's actual requirements?

areasOfAnalysis.title: Settings and config flow
areasOfAnalysis.question: How should shared defaults, Convex-stored operator policy, policy snapshots, and runtime-local secrets interact in the rewrite?

areasOfAnalysis.title: Monorepo package and runtime boundary
areasOfAnalysis.question: What package graph best separates Bun-friendly code, Convex code, and Node-only Temporal workers without recreating shared-runtime ambiguity?

areasOfAnalysis.title: Execution policy, rate limiting, and tooling boundary
areasOfAnalysis.question: Where should provider-facing throttling, adapter execution, and Temporal-facing tooling live, and what remains open about cross-worker global quotas?

areasOfAnalysis.title: Global provider quota strategy
areasOfAnalysis.question: How should Temporal queue shaping, worker saturation controls, and Redis-backed shared buckets combine to enforce provider/model request and token quotas across workers?

areasOfAnalysis.title: Provider capability divergence
areasOfAnalysis.question: What real differences across OpenAI, Anthropic, and Gemini must the rewrite model explicitly so the core runtime does not stay OpenAI-shaped?

areasOfAnalysis.title: Provider-portable code architecture
areasOfAnalysis.question: What registry-plus-adapter architecture keeps the Temporal rewrite maintainable as new providers are introduced without forcing every provider-specific feature into v0?

areasOfAnalysis.title: Minimal v0 capability registry schema
areasOfAnalysis.question: What minimal first-class registry fields should shared contracts expose so the core runtime, llm_attempts envelope, and Upstash keys stay provider-portable?

areasOfAnalysis.title: llm_attempts envelope and Upstash key model
areasOfAnalysis.question: What should be first-class in the generic attempt envelope, what should stay in provider_extensions, and how should Upstash keys derive from the normalized registry vocabulary?

areasOfAnalysis.title: V0 quota-dimension and tracking split
areasOfAnalysis.question: What normalized quota dimensions should v0 support, and which usage fields should stay tracked but not independently enforced?

areasOfAnalysis.title: Upstash v0 settlement policy
areasOfAnalysis.question: What should the default Upstash key form, algorithm choice, and reservation/reconciliation policy be for the first rollout?

areasOfAnalysis.title: Initial provider-to-dimension mapping
areasOfAnalysis.question: How should the first registry snapshot map OpenAI, Anthropic, and Gemini onto the normalized v0 quota dimensions without overfitting core code to one provider's billing model?

areasOfAnalysis.title: Provider-aware output reservation policy
areasOfAnalysis.question: How should output reservation differ across providers so the shared limiter stays conservative and faithful to documented quota semantics?

areasOfAnalysis.title: Final control contract
areasOfAnalysis.question: What exact primitive, acknowledgement mode, idempotency key, and fallback should each create/start/pause_after/pause/resume/cancel/repair action use?

areasOfAnalysis.title: llm_attempts schema and retention contract
areasOfAnalysis.question: What exact metadata, payload-ref, and retention/replay structure should replace the old runtime-shaped request tables?

areasOfAnalysis.title: Convex to Temporal worker API boundary
areasOfAnalysis.question: What is the narrowest stable API and package boundary that lets Temporal workers write durable domain results into Convex without recreating a mixed-runtime engine?

areasOfAnalysis.title: Observability projection and repair-read model
areasOfAnalysis.question: What compact projection should Convex mirror for UI and agent ergonomics, and what stronger Temporal surface must confirm any repair action?

areasOfAnalysis.title: Safe deployment and versioning SOP
areasOfAnalysis.question: What minimum replay, patching, continue-as-new, and rollout workflow should judge-gym require before shipping the Temporal rewrite?
